description: Machine-readable index of the Noumenal research atlas: the rendered page (sections, cards, witnesses) plus the complete verified corpus of the programme. Every proof entry names a real Lean declaration in the cited repository.

trust_base.note: verified modulo a named base; per-declaration axiom audits via #print_axioms

sections.name: Substrate
sections.def: What a hypothesis is made of — the representational medium a world-model lives in.

cards.title: TLT · Transformer Design Law
cards.author: Dhruv Gupta
cards.direction: A design law for transformers and neurosymbolic systems, proved in Lean 4 over measurability-theoretic foundations. The theory fixes when attention routing is universal, when a configuration is admissible, and it carries a certificate down to the literal IEEE-binary32 program the hardware runs.

subcards.title: Attention routing · foundations
subcards.direction: The open neurosymbolic-routing programme: where in a transformer does continuous, certifiable neural computation hand off to discrete symbolic routing, and what does an agent pay in learnability and design budget for crossing that line? The learner model, read along attention’s temperature axis. (Its settled certificate + expressivity theorems live in §06 Knowledge.)
subcards.note: Open programme. The cliff and design law are proved (the temperature instance of Gupta arXiv:2604.25028, Thm 4.1, at attention’s regularization boundary); what is open for the agent is using βStar and the config map as live controls, choosing routing temperature and architecture against a certified budget during self-improvement. Schematic figures redraw the repo’s assets/{cliff,crossover}.svg.

witnesses.claim: The measurability cliff, the centerpiece: a strict descriptive-complexity drop located exactly at attention’s softmax/argmax boundary. Soft (neural) attention’s bad event is Borel, learnable and certifiable; the hard (symbolic) argmax mixture-of-experts cascade is analytic-but-non-Borel at every depth, yet stays learnable as null-measurable. Routing temperature is the neural↔symbolic dial.

witnesses.code:
theorem measurabilityCliff :
    soft multi-head stack ⊨ KW (Borel ghost-gap);
    hard argmax MoE cascade ⊭ KW, yet null-measurable.
    Strict KW ⊊ WB_meas at the softmax/argmax boundary
theorem temperatureCliff : the drop is the routing temperature

witnesses.claim: Why the agent should care: measurability is the certificate’s precondition. The executed fp32 forward is measurable, so the §06 bound applies; for the argmax cascade no measurable functional represents the bad event, so the precondition is unsatisfiable exactly where the cliff falls, the symbolic regime forfeits the certificate the neural regime earns.
witnesses.code:
theorem executedCertificate_precondition_satisfied_and_violated :
    executed fp32 forward is measurable  (hFmeas holds, bound applies)
    ∧  argmax cascade: no measurable functional  (hFmeas unsatisfiable)

witnesses.claim: The tempered design law the agent optimizes over: two complementary certificates bound the same routed-symbol gap, a smooth Dudley certificate, tighter when routing is soft; a hard arrangement-VC Sauer–Shelah certificate, tighter when routing is sharp, meeting at an explicit crossover sharpness βStar on the real attention router. βStar is where an agent should place its routing temperature.

The handle this gives an agent: the certified capacity is a map out of architecture-configuration space, so every TransformerConfig (four Nats) carries a generalization budget, the design law is capacity ⊕ measurability ⊕ expressivity, and self-improvement is search over that map.
witnesses.code:
theorem config_capacity_law :
    ∀ cfg : TransformerConfig, certified budget(cfg)
    -- depth & head count flow through the Dudley capacity term
theorem TransformerDesignLaw : capacity ⊕ measurability ⊕ expressivity

subcards.title: Certified Execution
subcards.direction: The generalization bound is proved for the transformer program as it executes in IEEE binary32 arithmetic, with the certified attention head bound coordinate-for-coordinate to the literal scaledDotProductAttention kernel. Rounding error enters through a backward-error bound for binary32 sequential summation, derived rather than assumed, and propagates through non-expansive layer stacks with an envelope that grows linearly in depth, not exponentially.
subcards.note: The linear depth envelope is stated for stacks whose layers have Lipschitz constant at most one and per-layer rounding within a fixed budget.

witnesses.claim: End-to-end certified generalization bound for the transformer as executed in IEEE binary32 arithmetic.
witnesses.code:
theorem certified_executed_generalization_dudley [IsProbabilityMeasure P] {d : ℕ} [Nonempty (Fin d)]
    (hm : 0 < m) {R : ℝ} (hR : 0 ≤ R) (F : ParamSpace d → V → ℝ) {b : ℝ} (hb : 0 < b)

witnesses.claim: The certified attention head is bound to the literal scaledDotProductAttention kernel.
witnesses.code:
theorem attnHead_executed_certified_generalization {n d p m : ℕ} [NeZero n] [Nonempty (Fin p)]
    [MeasurableSpace (Fin n → Fin d → ℝ)] [BorelSpace (Fin n → Fin d → ℝ)]

witnesses.claim: Backward-error bound for IEEE binary32 sequential summation, derived as a rounding atom for all block reductions.
witnesses.code:
theorem fp32Foldl_error_le_of_sum_bound (xs : List ℝ) (S : ℝ)
    (hnorm : Fp32FoldlNormal 0 xs)
    (hun : neuralBpow binaryRadix (-24) * (xs.length : ℝ) < 1)
    (hS : (xs.map (fun x => |x|)).sum ≤ S)

witnesses.claim: Non-expansive stacks accumulate rounding error linearly in depth, not exponentially.

subcards.title: The Measurability Cliff
subcards.direction: Softmax routing keeps the uniform-convergence bad events of attention Borel, while argmax routing produces bad events that are analytic but not Borel: a strict descriptive-complexity drop located at the softmax/argmax boundary, with routing temperature acting as the toggle at every depth. The dichotomy is governed by the parameter space: sigma-compact spaces admit no such pathology, while Polish non-sigma-compact spaces carry a binary attention router whose bad event is analytic and non-Borel. The same measurability condition is the precondition on which the positive executed certificates stand.
subcards.note: The non-Borel constructions take the classical existence of an analytic non-Borel set of reals as an explicit hypothesis.

witnesses.claim: Descriptive complexity drops strictly from Borel to analytic at the softmax/argmax boundary.
witnesses.code:
theorem measurabilityCliff
    {d : ℕ} {V : Type*} [MeasurableSpace V]
    (F : (Fin d → ℝ) → V → ℝ)
    (hFmeas : ∀ θ, Measurable (F θ)) (hFcont : ∀ x, Continuous fun θ : Fin d → ℝ => F θ x)

witnesses.claim: Temperature as a measurability toggle: soft routing is Borel, argmax routing is non-Borel at every depth.
witnesses.code:
theorem temperatureCliff {τ : ℝ} (hτ : 0 ≤ τ) (g : β → ℝ) (hg : Continuous g) [Nonempty β]
    (h_non_borel : ¬ MeasurableSet (Set.range g)) (L : ℕ)

witnesses.claim: Sigma-compact parameter spaces are tame; Polish non-sigma-compact spaces admit analytic non-Borel bad events.
witnesses.code:
theorem attention_router_measurability_dichotomy
    (hex : ∃ A : Set ℝ, AnalyticSet A ∧ ¬ MeasurableSet A) :
    (∀ (β : Type) [TopologicalSpace β] [SigmaCompactSpace β] (g : β → ℝ),
        Continuous g → MeasurableSet (Set.range g)
          ∧ MeasurableSet (singletonBadEvent (Set.range g)))

witnesses.claim: A binary attention router over a Polish parameter space whose uniform-convergence bad event is analytic but not Borel.
witnesses.code:

    (hex : ∃ A : Set ℝ, MeasureTheory.AnalyticSet A ∧ ¬ MeasurableSet A) :
    ∃ (β : Type) (_hτ : TopologicalSpace β) (_hP : PolishSpace β)

witnesses.claim: Measurability is the hinge between the positive certificate and the negative cliff.

subcards.title: Attention Capacity · Design Laws
subcards.direction: The capacity bound covers the full multi-head encoder stack, multi-head attention with feed-forward blocks, layer normalization, and residual connections, with depth-graded constants that are linear in head count and free of sequence length. The Dudley entropy-integral bound is discharged to a closed affine form with the optimal constant, and the Lipschitz constant for softmax attention on a bounded query/key ball does not depend on sequence length. The certified capacity bound is packaged as a design law: a map out of configuration space indexed by TransformerConfig.

witnesses.claim: Depth-graded generalization bound for the true multi-head encoder stack with H heads and L layers.
witnesses.code:
theorem transformerEncoderStackMH_certified_generalization {H p m : ℕ} [NeZero n] [Nonempty (Fin p)]
    [MeasurableSpace (Fin n → Fin d → ℝ)] [BorelSpace (Fin n → Fin d → ℝ)]
    {P : Measure (Fin n → Fin d → ℝ)} [IsProbabilityMeasure P] (hm : 0 < m)

witnesses.claim: Architecture design law: certified capacity as a map out of configuration space, indexed by TransformerConfig.
witnesses.code:
theorem config_capacity_law (cfg : TransformerConfig) {p m : ℕ} [NeZero n] [Nonempty (Fin p)]
    [MeasurableSpace (Fin n → Fin cfg.embedDim → ℝ)] [BorelSpace (Fin n → Fin cfg.embedDim → ℝ)]

subcards.title: Transformers · TLT (design-lab)
subcards.direction: The transformer library inside the verified corpus.

witnesses.claim: The result: a generalization bound stated about the executed program, not an idealized model, every term computable from the actual fp32 weights, off a McDiarmid-small sample event.
witnesses.code:
theorem certified_executed_generalization_dudley :
    R_trueᵉˣᵉᶜ ≤ R̂_empᵉˣᵉᶜ
      + 2·(12√2·B/√m) + ε + 2·L·envBound
  -- B the affine Dudley entropy integral; envBound the fp32 envelope

theorem attnHead_literal_certified_generalization :
    the bound holds for TorchLean’s literal IEEE32Exec
    scaledDotProductAttention — the rounding atom is derived,
    not supplied (no input-quantization slack)

witnesses.claim: One network, read two ways: exact reals for the theorems and bit-exact float32 for the program, proven equal in coordinates, the certified head is TorchLean’s literal scaledDotProductAttention, and the head-split reshape carries no pathology.
witnesses.code:
theorem matCoords_scaledDotProductAttention :
    certifiedHead = Spec.scaledDotProductAttention  (in coordinates)
theorem multiHeadAttention_forward_one :
    Spec.MultiHeadAttention.forward (1 head) = reshape selfAttn

witnesses.claim: It lifts to the real architecture: a depth-L stack of true multi-head encoder layers (attention ∘ feed-forward), capacity growing with depth and head count and free of any sequence-length factor.
witnesses.code:
theorem transformerEncoderStackMH_certified_generalization :
    certified fp32 bound for a depth-L multi-head encoder stack
theorem multiHeadAttn_weight_lip :
    MHA Lipschitz in weights, constant linear in H, no seq-length factor

witnesses.claim: The expressivity hierarchy: depth and width strictly buy capability, proved as real non-membership separations at every rung, and the routing’s logical type is read off the score degree (affine = first-order convex cells; self-attention’s quadratic scores = second-order semialgebraic, non-convex, yet still finite-VC).

witnesses.code:

theorem constArityGrade_ssubset_succ : arity n ⊊ arity (n+1)  ∀n  (width)
theorem affineArgmaxCell_FO / quadraticArgmaxCell_SO_semialgebraic :
    affine routing = first-order; quadratic (self-attn) = second-order

cards.title: TorchLean
cards.author: Robert Joseph et al.
cards.direction: A Lean 4 framework for writing, running, inspecting, and verifying neural-network programs: typed tensors and model APIs, a shared graph IR, runtime and autograd support, finite-precision semantics, certificate checkers, and CUDA runtime boundaries, with examples across modern and scientific ML.

cards.title: Design Lab
cards.author: Dhruv Gupta
cards.direction: The largest formalized machine-learning corpus in the world: six verified Lean 4 libraries over one pinned toolchain, 17,127 indexed declarations measured 2026-06-15, about 3,700 of them in-house, every result carrying a sorry count and an axiom audit against the named base. Each library below is a region of the same connected corpus.

witnesses.claim: The module dependency hypergraph of the corpus: one connected object, not a folder of files.
witnesses.alt: Module dependency hypergraph of the design-lab corpus, a single connected graph across the six libraries.

witnesses.headline: 17,127 indexed declarations across six verified libraries, about 3,700 in-house, measured 2026-06-15. The learning-theory core alone is 354 machine-checked theorems with zero sorry, on the axiom base {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}.

subcards.title: Learning Theory · FLT
subcards.direction: The formalized core of statistical learning theory: the five-way fundamental theorem, VC dimension, compression, PAC-Bayes, and the measurability conditions under which learnability is well defined.

witnesses.claim: Learner measurability is the precondition for being an admissible measurable policy: MeasurableBatchLearner is a Lean 4 typeclass for joint learner measurability, and the kernel proves a strict measurability hierarchy, discrete and symbolic learners are always measurable; uncountable selection can leave a learner outside the Borel σ-algebra yet inside the null-measurable one.

witnesses.code:
class MeasurableBatchLearner (X C) where
    learn_measurable : a jointly-measurable learned rule stays
      measurable in future records once training is fixed
theorem measurability_hierarchy_strict :
    Measurable ⊊ NullMeasurable  (inhabited by an FLT class)

witnesses.claim: The σ-algebra refinement: the kernel’s uniform-convergence condition (WellBehavedVC, requiring only NullMeasurableSet) is strictly weaker than Krapp–Wirth (2024), which requires MeasurableSet (Borel), a modern σ-algebra-explicit form of the Pollard–Dudley universal-measurability framework, packaged as a Borel-parameterization typeclass.
witnesses.code:
instance : WellBehavedVCMeasTarget C
  -- any class parameterized by a StandardBorelSpace with jointly
  -- measurable evaluation; needs NullMeasurableSet, not Borel
theorem wellBehavedVC_weaker_than_krappWirth

witnesses.claim: The Borel-analytic separation, the standout: the first concrete concept-class witness inhabiting the strict gap between an analytic-non-Borel bad event and a Borel one. WellBehavedVC holds (the bad event is null-measurable) while Krapp–Wirth fails (it is not Borel). The full counterexample proof is below.
witnesses.alt: Three-panel Borel-analytic separation: the singleton witness class over an analytic non-Borel set A; the proof chain bad-event → Suslin projection → analytic → Choquet capacity → null-measurable, against the failed assume-Borel path; and the strict gap WellBehavedVC HOLDS vs KrappWirth FAILS.

witnesses.code:
-- The entire Borel-analytic counterexample (exists_measTarget_separation)

WITNESS.   Let A ⊆ ℝ be analytic, non-Borel (exists under
  standard set theory). Define the singleton class
      singletonClassOn(A) = {zeroConcept} ∪ {singletonConcept a | a ∈ A}
  where singletonConcept a returns true only at x = a.

STEP 1  (every hypothesis is Borel).
  zeroConcept is constant; each singletonConcept a is piecewise
  constant on the measurable singleton {a}.  So every hypothesis in
  the class is Borel-measurable.

STEP 2  (reduce to a planar witness).
  The uniform-convergence bad event — ghost-sample pairs on which
  some hypothesis has empirical gap ≥ ε/2 — reduces, by a preimage
  calculation, to the planar witness event W ⊆ ℝ².

STEP 3  (W is analytic ⇒ null-measurable).
  W = { (training, ghost) : ∃ a ∈ A. training misses a ∧ ghost hits a }.
  W is an existential projection of a Borel set, hence analytic (Σ¹₁)
  by Suslin's theorem (1917).  Analytic sets are universally
  measurable by Choquet capacitability (1954; Kechris 30.13, formalized
  here) — so W is NullMeasurableSet.            ⊢ WellBehavedVCMeasTarget

STEP 4  (W is not Borel).
  Suppose W were Borel.  Then its preimage under the constant section
      x ↦ (a₀, x)
  would be Borel.  But that preimage is A itself — not Borel. ⇒⇐
                                                 ⊬ KrappWirthWellBehaved

CONCLUSION.
  WellBehavedVCMeasTarget holds while KrappWirthWellBehaved fails:
  the gap between NullMeasurableSet and MeasurableSet is inhabited by
  a concrete concept class.                      ∎  (axioms: propext,
                                                    Classical.choice, Quot.sound)

witnesses.claim: Why an agent needs this: BorelRouterCode abstracts conditional interpolation, attention, mixture-of-experts routing, and transfer, as Borel-parameterized concept classes, so the measurability guarantee transports to the routed/neurosymbolic learners of §04. Interpolation and amalgamation closures are corollaries.
witnesses.code:
def BorelRouterCode : conditional interpolation over a
    StandardBorelSpace — attention · routing · transfer
theorem interpClassFixed_subset_amalgClass :
    interpolation ⊆ amalgamation  (both inherit WellBehavedVCMeasTarget)

subcards.title: Measurements · ZPM
subcards.direction: The self-measurement library: divergences and independence criteria (Pinsker, MMD, HSIC, mutual information), Choquet capacity, and concentration.

witnesses.code:
theorem pinsker_proof (P Q : Measure α)
    [IsProbabilityMeasure P] [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    (hac : P.AbsolutelyContinuous Q) :
    tvDistReal P Q ≤ Real.sqrt (klDivReal P Q / 2)

witnesses.claim: Pinsker's inequality with sharp constant, fully proved: total variation is at most the square root of half the KL divergence.
witnesses.code:
theorem pinsker_proof
    (P Q : Measure α) [IsProbabilityMeasure P] [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    (hac : P.AbsolutelyContinuous Q) (h_int : Integrable (llr P Q) P) :
    tvDistReal P Q ≤ Real.sqrt (klDivReal P Q / 2)

witnesses.claim: For a characteristic kernel, MMD vanishes exactly when the two distributions are equal.
witnesses.code:
theorem mmdSq_zero_iff
    (hchar : IsCharacteristic (H := H) (X := X))
    (P Q : Measure X) :
    mmdSq (H := H) P Q = 0 ↔ P = Q

witnesses.claim: For a characteristic kernel on the product space, HSIC vanishes exactly when the joint distribution is the product of its marginals.
witnesses.code:
theorem hsicDef_zero_iff_independent
    (hchar : IsCharacteristic (H := H) (X := X × Y))
    (P : Measure (X × Y)) :
    hsicDef (H := H) P = 0 ↔ P = (P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)

witnesses.claim: Mutual information equals zero if and only if the variables are independent, under an absolute continuity hypothesis.
witnesses.code:
theorem mutualInformationReal_eq_zero_iff_product {α β : Type*}
    [MeasurableSpace α] [MeasurableSpace β]
    (P : Measure (α × β)) [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    (hac : P.AbsolutelyContinuous ((P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)))
    (hfin : InformationTheory.klDiv P ((P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)) ≠ ⊤) :
    mutualInformationReal P = 0 ↔ P = (P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)

witnesses.claim: The reproducing property of the kernel mean embedding: inner products against the embedding compute expectations.
witnesses.code:
theorem kernelMeanEmbedding_reproducing
    (P : Measure X) [IsFiniteMeasure P]
    (hcont : Continuous (fun x => kerFun H x (1 : ℝ)))
    (f : H) :
    @inner ℝ H _ (kernelMeanEmbedding (H := H) P) f = ∫ x, f x ∂P

subcards.title: Reinforcement Learning · RLTheory
subcards.direction: Stochastic approximation and value learning: almost-sure convergence of Q-learning and TD under Markovian sampling, on a shared Lyapunov / Robbins-Siegmund backbone.

witnesses.code:

    ∀ᵐ ω, Tendsto (fun n ↦ Q n ω) atTop
      (𝓝 spec.optimalQ)

witnesses.claim: Q-learning converges almost surely to the optimal Q-function under Markovian samples.
witnesses.code:

witnesses.claim: The Robbins-Siegmund almost-supermartingale theorem, the foundation of the stochastic approximation proofs.
witnesses.code:
theorem ae_tendsto_zero_of_almost_supermartingale
    (hfnonneg : ∀ n, 0 ≤ᵐ[μ] f n)
    {T : ℕ → ℝ} (hTpos : ∀ n, 0 < T n)
    (hAlmostSupermartingale :
      ∃ C ≥ 0, ∀ n, μ[f (n + 1) | ℱ n] ≤ᵐ[μ] (fun ω => (1 - T n) * f n ω + C * T n ^ 2)) :
    ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, Tendsto (fun n => f n ω) atTop (𝓝 0)

witnesses.claim: A Lyapunov-based recursive error bound shared by all the convergence proofs.
witnesses.code:
theorem fundamental_inequality
  {x : ℕ → Ω → E d}
  (hx : Iterates x x₀ f e₁ e₂ α)
  ...
  [LyapunovFunction φ φ' f] :
  ∃ B₁ B₂, 0 < B₁ ∧ 0 ≤ B₂ ∧ ∃ n₀, ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, ∀ n, n ≥ n₀ →
    φ (x (n + 1) ω - z) ≤ (1 - B₁ * α n) * φ (x n ω - z) +
      ⟪φ' (x n ω - z), e₁ (n + 1) ω⟫ + B₂ * (α n) ^ 2

witnesses.claim: Smoothness of the squared l_p norm with constant p - 1, the Lyapunov candidate for Q-learning.
witnesses.code:
theorem smooth_half_sq_Lp (hp : 2 ≤ p) :
  ∀ (x y : LpSpace p d),
    half_sq_Lp y ≤ half_sq_Lp x + ⟪toL2 (half_sq_Lp' x), toL2 (y - x)⟫ + (p - 1) * half_sq_Lp (y - x)

subcards.title: Neural Networks · TorchLean
subcards.direction: Certified neural-network execution: typed tensors, a verified graph IR, finite-precision semantics, and generalization certificates that bind to the literal executed program.

witnesses.alt: Interval-bound propagation and alpha-CROWN pipeline: a perturbation set propagated through affine and ReLU relaxations to certified output bounds.

witnesses.claim: ReLU is 1-Lipschitz in L2 for any tensor shape, proved by structural induction on the shape type.
witnesses.code:

  tensorL2Dist (reluSpec x) (reluSpec y) ≤ tensorL2Dist x y

witnesses.claim: A per-step squared-norm contraction bound for gradient steps on strongly monotone Lipschitz operators.
witnesses.code:
theorem step_norm_sq_le (η μ : ℝ) (hη : 0 ≤ η) {L : NNReal} (g : E → E)
    (hmono : StrongMonotone μ g) (hlip : LipschitzWith L g)
    (x y : E) :
    ‖step η g x - step η g y‖ ^ 2 ≤ (1 - 2 * η * μ + (η ^ 2) * (L : ℝ) ^ 2) * ‖x - y‖ ^ 2

witnesses.claim: Uniform stability of one-dimensional ridge regression with an explicit stability constant.
witnesses.code:
theorem ridgeFit1D_sqLoss_uniformStableReplace (hlam : 0 < lam) :
    UniformStableReplace (Z := BoundedExample X Y) (H := ℝ)
      (A := fun S => ridgeFit1D (n := n) (X := X) (Y := Y) lam S)
      (ℓ := fun w z => sqLoss (X := X) (Y := Y) w z)
      (β := 4 * X ^ 2 * Y ^ 2 * (lam + X ^ 2) ^ 2 / (lam ^ 3 * N (n := n)))

cards.title: Causal world model · causalWM
cards.author: Chethan
cards.direction: A causal world model: object-centric slots, an explicitly inferred directed interaction graph, and latent-space rollouts where one scalar per object pair gates all cross-object influence. Because the structure is declared in the model rather than left implicit, interventions and counterfactuals become checkable: cut an edge and the rollout changes.
cards.note: Experiments across graviton, PushT, PHYRE, and CLEVRER; figures below are the project's own report figures. One causal core with pluggable perception per dataset, trained in two stages.

witnesses.claim: The shared causal core: a per-slot encoder, a confounder-free mechanism-inference module, and per-object mechanisms whose cross-object influence is gated by one inferred adjacency scalar per pair.
witnesses.alt: Architecture of the causalWM shared causal core on graviton: a per-slot encoder feeds a confounder-free mechanism-inference module that emits per-object mechanisms, gated by an inferred adjacency matrix, and rolls the future forward through that graph.

witnesses.headline: On the controlled graviton benchmark, where structure is scored against a planted ground-truth graph, adjacency AUROC is 1.0 and recoverable structural Hamming distance is 0 on every seed. Structure and confounder recovery hold from 100% down to 10% of the training data.

sections.name: Measurement
sections.def: Numbers the scientist can compute on itself — and a progress meter that does not bend under pressure.

cards.title: Witness-cost learnability
cards.author: Ayush Mittal
cards.direction: A guarantee is epistemically hollow without a checkable witness: learnability is witness-limited. The program defines WitCost, the minimum witness information needed to certify a frame-relative improvement under calibration, proves an exchange law in clean regimes, and validates a non-circular instrument that measures the cost and can falsify it, including a measured lift where an interventional frame certifies what an observational frame provably cannot.
cards.note: Claims are stratified proved / measured / open in the repository's reviewer guide; a proved spectral converse for the identified general functional landed 2026-07-05.

sections.name: Agents
sections.def: The loop that proposes and probes — learning, imagined experiments, and intervention under uncertainty.

cards.title: noumenal_agent
cards.author: Amit Timalsina · Ayush Mittal
cards.direction: AI that runs operations: actions are the output, multimodal data (multivariate time series plus video) is the input, and process and control understanding is the substrate. The bar is important actions done at high accuracy and usefulness.
cards.note: Private production monorepo; the runtime drives ingest, run, poll, and steer flows end to end.

cards.title: causalWM · interventions at rollout
cards.author: Chethan
cards.direction: The agent-facing face of the causal world model: rolling futures forward in latent space under interventions, with cross-object influence carried only through the inferred graph, so an edge can be knocked out and the effect read off the rollout.
cards.note: The report includes the parts that did not work; the numbers below are from the project's experiments.

witnesses.claim: The graph is load-bearing: the planted true adjacency beside the learned soft adjacency and the edge-knockout delta-MSE. If the adjacency were decorative, knocking out an edge would move nothing.
witnesses.alt: graviton adjacency and edge-knockout: the planted true adjacency, the raw soft adjacency the model learns (close to a uniform gate), and the directed edge-knockout delta-MSE which is sparse and recovers the true structure.

witnesses.headline: The adjacency is causal, not decorative: cutting all edges raises rollout error 8.54 times, the action's effect localizes to the agent slot (0.114 against 0.03 elsewhere), and reversing the action diverges the trajectory. Cutting an edge damages only the child objects, which is what a faithful causal model must do.

The honest reversal, reported as the sharpest finding: on PushT the model is the more stable long-horizon predictor (0.00842 error at ten steps while the baseline drifts) yet it plans worse than the baseline it beats. The failure is representational, not dynamical, and falsifiable: the slots miss the block pose by at least 20 pixels, the planning success tolerance.

A negative result, banked with numbers: on CLEVRER the inferred graph does not detect collisions. The causal-probe AUROC sits at or below chance and relational-knockout at collisions (AUROC 0.36) is barely distinguishable from knocking out edges anywhere.

witnesses.claim: PHYRE, at pixel scale: ground truth, the model's rollout, and the underlying slots for one episode.
witnesses.alt: PHYRE filmstrip: ground truth, model rollout, and the underlying slots for one episode, at pixel scale.

cards.title: noumenal-edge
cards.author: Ayush Mittal
cards.direction: The edge runtime for autonomous industrial operations: read the world, run a signed policy, check the safety envelope, dispatch an action, and prove what happened, over a signed, deterministic, append-only event log with a tick-loop kernel.

cards.title: noumenal_api
cards.author: Amit Timalsina

cards.title: World Models · Build your world

sections.name: Reasoning
sections.def: Deriving the certain and generalizing the uncertain — and auditing the path that got there.

cards.title: AgentState
cards.author: Ayush Mittal
cards.direction: A clear reasoning target: hash-chained agent state with four verification layers and claim-checking, so every committed state is independently re-checkable.
cards.note: Rust / Go — a code witness, not Lean. Witness form to decide.

cards.title: RSI · Verified Self-Improvement
cards.author: Dhruv Gupta
cards.direction: Recursive self-improvement admitted only under a hard verifier: a sealed audit whose reward the agent provably cannot game. Three papers make the guarantee precise, from the progress signal to its anytime certificate to its counterfactual wrapper.

subcards.title: Compression Progress · Goodhart-resistant sealed audit
subcards.direction: Signed compression progress on a sealed audit telescopes to an endpoint difference, so cumulative reward cannot exceed true audit improvement by more than a finite budget. The first faithful compression-progress target.

witnesses.claim: Telescoping identity: signed audit compression progress is exactly Goodhart-resistant.
witnesses.code:
theorem cumCP_telescope {ι : Type*} (E : ι → ℝ) (g : ℕ → ι) (T : ℕ) :
    cumCP E g T = E (g 0) - E (g T)

witnesses.claim: Finite budget: cumulative signed progress is bounded whenever the audit loss stays above a floor.
witnesses.code:
theorem cumCP_le_of_lb {ι : Type*} (E : ι → ℝ) (g : ℕ → ι) (T : ℕ) {Emin : ℝ}
    (hlb : Emin ≤ E (g T)) : cumCP E g T ≤ E (g 0) - Emin

witnesses.claim: Finite-panel deviation budget 2*Delta is horizon-free by endpoint reduction.
Conditional entropy floor: progress on noisy components is bounded by the initial excess over H(Y|X).
witnesses.code:
theorem cond_entropy_floor_budget_crossEntropy (μ : FinitePMF X) (Q : X → FinitePMF H)
    (P : ι → X → FinitePMF H) [hP : ∀ θ, ∀ x, HasPositivePrior (P θ x)] (g : ℕ → ι) (T : ℕ) :
    cumCP (fun θ => condCrossEntropyFinitePMF μ Q (P θ)) g T
      ≤ condCrossEntropyFinitePMF μ Q (P (g 0)) - condEntropyFinitePMF μ Q

theorem entropy_le_crossEntropyFinitePMF {H : Type*} [Fintype H]
    (Q P : FinitePMF H) [HasPositivePrior P] :
    entropyFinitePMF Q ≤ crossEntropyFinitePMF Q P

subcards.title: PAC-Bayes Self-Modifying
subcards.direction: A PAC-Bayes bound and an anytime-certificate format for self-improving agents, demonstrated on a continually self-improving language agent: the certificate holds at every stopping time, not just in expectation.

subcards.title: Conformal Counterfactuals
subcards.direction: A conformal world-model wrapper with calibrated coverage on counterfactual rollouts: a formalization spec and Lean 4 development plan, with an Atari-100k and DMControl claim package.
subcards.note: Preprint; not yet started in Lean.

sections.name: Knowledge
sections.def: What is actually known, laid over the frontier.

cards.title: World-Model Theory
cards.author: Dhruv Gupta
cards.direction: A specification theory of world models, machine-checked in Lean 4: declare what a latent is for, a target family, a readout budget, and a dynamics law, and the theory determines which distinctions the latent must keep, which transitions descend to it, and which representation is forced. Developed across eight arguments, from the readout characterization to a scoped conclusion for JEPA-class systems.
cards.note: Every claim on the companion site is a theorem in Lean 4: noumenal-ai.github.io/world-model-theory.

cards.title: causal-worlds
cards.author: Amit Timalsina
cards.direction: Turn a plain-language description of an operation into a fictional but coherent causal world with a declared ground-truth causal graph, then benchmark whether a causal-discovery method can recover it. Because the structure is declared, not learned, every generated world is an answer key: run any discovery method on the data and score how well it recovered the world.
cards.note: Published on PyPI with CI, MIT licensed. The witnesses below are a live run on a generated world from the shipped benchmark.

witnesses.claim: The declared ground-truth causal DAG of a generated coffee-chain world, rendered by the package itself: eight variables across disturbance, controllable, observable and outcome roles, the hidden confounder dashed.
witnesses.alt: Declared ground-truth causal DAG of a generated regional-coffee-chain world: eight variables (weekend, loyalty discount, supplier lead time, foot traffic, queue length, drinks sold, daily margin, and a hidden barista-morale confounder), with directed causal edges and the hidden confounder drawn dashed.
witnesses.source: causal-worlds · benchmark/v0.2/world_00 · rendered with viz.to_dot + graphviz

witnesses.headline: On a generated coffee-chain world (8 variables, 9 ground-truth edges, one hidden confounder, 2000 rows), an interventional grader recovers the graph at F1 0.88 and keeps zero spurious confounded edges; observational PC reaches F1 0.84 but keeps the hidden-confounded pair as a causal edge.

proofs.supports: Pinsker's inequality with the sharp constant: TV(P,Q) ≤ √(KL(P‖Q)/2)

proofs.signature:
theorem binary_pinsker (p q : ℝ) (hp₀ : 0 ≤ p) (hp₁ : p ≤ 1)
    (hq₀ : 0 < q) (hq₁ : q < 1) :
    2 * (p - q)^2 ≤ klBin p q
proofs.supports: Binary Pinsker inequality with sharp constant 2, the core lemma driving the measure-theoretic Pinsker proof

proofs.signature:
theorem mmdSq_zero_iff
    (hchar : IsCharacteristic (H := H) (X := X))
    (P Q : Measure X) :
    mmdSq (H := H) P Q = 0 ↔ P = Q
proofs.supports: MMD² metrizes equality of probability measures under a characteristic kernel (Sriperumbudur et al. JMLR 11, 2010, Theorem 8)

proofs.signature:
theorem hsicDef_zero_iff_independent
    (hchar : IsCharacteristic (H := H) (X := X × Y))
    (P : Measure (X × Y)) :
    hsicDef (H := H) P = 0 ↔ P = (P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)
proofs.supports: HSIC vanishes iff the joint distribution equals the product of marginals (statistical independence) under a characteristic kernel

proofs.signature:
theorem mwu_approx_minimax
    {R C : Type*} [Fintype R] [Fintype C] [Nonempty R] [Nonempty C]
    [DecidableEq R] [DecidableEq C]
    (M : R → C → Bool) (v ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε)
    (hrow : ∀ q : FintypePMF C, ∃ r : R,
      v ≤ ∑ c, q.prob c * (if M r c then (1 : ℝ) else 0)) :
    ∃ p : FintypePMF R, ∀ c : C, v - ε ≤ boolGamePayoff M p c
proofs.supports: MWU approximate minimax: T = O(log N / ε²) rounds suffice to produce a mixed strategy achieving payoff ≥ v − ε against all pure columns

proofs.signature:
theorem assouad_transpose_vcDim {m n : ℕ} (M : BinaryMatrix m n)
    {d : ℕ} (hd : M.vcDim ≤ d) :
    M.transpose.vcDim ≤ 2 ^ (d + 1) - 1
proofs.supports: Assouad's dual VC bound: VCDim(Mᵀ) ≤ 2^{d+1} − 1 when VCDim(M) ≤ d

proofs.signature:
theorem card_toFinsetFamily_le {m n : ℕ} (M : BinaryMatrix m n)
    {d : ℕ} (hd : M.toFinsetFamily.vcDim ≤ d) :
    M.toFinsetFamily.card ≤ ∑ k ∈ Finset.Iic d, n.choose k
proofs.supports: Sauer–Shelah bound on the size of a set family with bounded VC dimension

proofs.signature:
lemma ProbabilityTheory.chebyshev_majority_bound
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : MeasureTheory.Measure Ω}
    [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {k : ℕ} {δ : ℝ} (h_delta_pos : 0 < δ)
    (hk : (9 : ℝ) / δ ≤ k)
    (events : Fin k → Set Ω)
    (hevents_meas : ∀ j, MeasurableSet (events j))
    (hindep : ProbabilityTheory.iIndepSet (fun j => events j) μ)
    (hprob : ∀ j, μ (events j) ≥ ENNReal.ofReal (2/3)) :
    μ {ω | k < 2 * (Finset.univ.filter (fun j => ω ∈ events j)).card} ≥
      ENNReal.ofReal (1 - δ)
proofs.supports: Chebyshev majority bound: k ≥ 9/δ independent events each with probability ≥ 2/3 gives majority with probability ≥ 1−δ

proofs.signature:
theorem kernelMeanEmbedding_reproducing
    (P : Measure X) [IsFiniteMeasure P]
    (hcont : Continuous (fun x => kerFun H x (1 : ℝ)))
    (f : H) :
    @inner ℝ H _ (kernelMeanEmbedding (H := H) P) f = ∫ x, f x ∂P
proofs.supports: Reproducing property of the kernel mean embedding: ⟨μ_P, f⟩ = ∫ f dP

proofs.signature:
theorem mutualInformationReal_eq_zero_iff_product {α β : Type*}
    [MeasurableSpace α] [MeasurableSpace β]
    (P : Measure (α × β)) [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    (hac : P.AbsolutelyContinuous ((P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)))
    (hfin : InformationTheory.klDiv P ((P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)) ≠ ⊤) :
    mutualInformationReal P = 0 ↔ P = (P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)
proofs.supports: Mutual information vanishes iff joint distribution equals product of marginals (statistical independence)
